best_for: Hardest reasoning: complex agentic coding, multi-step planning, high-stakes analysis where quality dominates cost
avoid_when: High-volume simple tasks; strict latency budgets; cost-sensitive features
tradeoffs: Highest quality and highest cost per token; overkill for classification/extraction

best_for: Flagship general work: coding, long-document analysis, agent loops when Fable access/cost is a constraint
tradeoffs: Strong quality just below Fable; supports fast-output mode in some products

best_for: The default product workhorse: chat features, summarization, RAG answers, structured extraction with good quality/cost balance
avoid_when: The task provably needs frontier reasoning, or is trivial enough for Haiku
tradeoffs: Best cost/quality ratio for most SaaS features; start here and move up/down on evidence

best_for: High-volume low-latency: classification, routing, autocomplete, moderation pre-filter, title generation
avoid_when: Nuanced reasoning, long multi-step agent loops
tradeoffs: Cheapest and fastest; quality drops on complex instructions — pair with a tiered fallback

best_for: Products with mixed workloads: route simple requests to Haiku and escalate hard ones to Sonnet/Opus by rule or classifier
avoid_when: A single uniform workload where one model fits all
tradeoffs: Big cost savings at the price of routing logic to build, test, and monitor

best_for: Math, planning, hard debugging, multi-constraint decisions where visible reasoning improves accuracy
avoid_when: Latency-sensitive chat; simple lookups — thinking tokens cost money and time
tradeoffs: Higher accuracy on hard tasks; slower and pricier per request; budget thinking tokens explicitly

best_for: Document understanding (invoices, receipts, forms), screenshot analysis, image-grounded Q&A
avoid_when: Pure-text products; OCR-only needs where a dedicated OCR API is cheaper at scale
tradeoffs: One API for text+image simplifies architecture; large images consume many tokens — resize first

best_for: RAG retrieval, semantic search, clustering, dedup — Anthropic has no embeddings API; use Voyage or similar
avoid_when: Tiny corpora where keyword/full-text search already works
tradeoffs: Adds a second vendor; pick one embedding model and version it — mixing models breaks the index

avoid_when: No dedicated ML-ops capacity; quality-sensitive features — hosted frontier models lead
tradeoffs: Full data control; you own GPU cost, scaling, safety tuning, and a lower quality ceiling

best_for: Long stable system prompts, big tool definitions, multi-turn chats replaying history each call
avoid_when: Prompts that change every call (cache misses cost slightly more than no cache)
tradeoffs: Up to ~90% input-cost cut and faster TTFT on hits; requires stable prompt prefixes — order matters

best_for: Asynchronous bulk jobs: nightly summarization, backfills, eval runs, bulk classification
avoid_when: Anything interactive — results return within hours, not seconds
tradeoffs: ~50% cost discount for giving up immediacy; design jobs as idempotent and resumable

best_for: Machine-readable results: extraction, form filling, API-consumable JSON — define a tool schema and force its use
avoid_when: Free-form creative text where a schema adds nothing
tradeoffs: Reliable parseable output; still validate server-side — schema conformance is not business-rule validity

best_for: Long-running chats approaching context limits; cost control on replayed history
avoid_when: Short sessions that never approach the window
tradeoffs: Keeps cost flat as conversations grow; summarization can drop details users expect the bot to remember

option: Claude Agent SDK vs raw API
best_for: Full agent products (tool loops, file operations, subagents) — the SDK ships the loop, permissions, and context management
avoid_when: A single prompt-in/answer-out feature — the raw Messages API is one HTTP call
tradeoffs: SDK saves rebuilding agent plumbing; raw API keeps the dependency surface minimal
